FT_IC1.evidence: Uses ABCD Study data: 3498 U.S. adolescents aged 9-11 years from Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen time measured as generic total composite (hours/day) from ABCD Youth Screen Time Survey covering TV, videos, games, texting, video chat, social media combined. No separately reported phone/SM coefficient. Violates FT-IC2 requirement for specific smartphone OR social media variable. Per criteria: 'Generic total screen time or STS composite with no phone/SM breakdown' = NOT_MET.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article in International Journal of Obesity, 2025

FT_EC7_reason: FT-IC2 NOT_MET; does not reach criteria check for FT-EC7
notes: Screen time is generic composite; fails FT-IC2. Resolver agrees with C1.

FT_IC1.evidence: Uses ABCD Study Year 2 (2018-2020), N=8753 children aged 10-14 years

FT_IC2.evidence: Measures three specific SM/phone variables: Video Game Addiction Questionnaire (VGAQ), Social Media Addiction Questionnaire (SMAQ), Mobile Phone Involvement Questionnaire (MPIQ). All are problematic-use scales qualifying under FT-IC2.

FT_IC3.evidence: Multiple linear regression analyses linking problematic phone/SM use (DVs) to sociodemographic factors (IVs). Table 3 reports associations across all three screen use measures with test statistics.

FT_IC4.evidence: Table 3 reports B coefficients with 95% CIs and p-values (e.g., Male vs Female: B=0.74, 95% CI 0.69-0.79, p<0.001 for video games; B=-0.11, 95% CI -0.16 to -0.05, p<0.001 for social media)

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical cross-sectional analysis of ABCD Year 2 data with hypothesis testing and reported associations across three problematic use measures

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article (Pediatric Research, 2022); no evidence of duplicate/preprint

FT_EC7_reason: Cross-sectional design. FT-EC7 applies ONLY when ALL analyses are longitudinal/prospective. Criteria state: 'Paper also reports cross-sectional analyses with smartphone/SM as DV (cross-sectional ignores direction)' → FT-EC7 does NOT apply. Resolver agrees with C2.
notes: Paper explicitly analyzes cross-sectional Year 2 data. Although phone/SM are DVs with demographic-only predictors, FT-EC7 requires longitudinal design. This is cross-sectional demographic→SM analysis, which is INCLUDE per FT-EC7 exclusion rule.

FT_IC1.evidence: Uses ABCD Study baseline data (ages 9-10 at baseline) from 10,000+ youth across 21 U.S. sites

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen time mentioned as one predictor among many in multi-domain machine learning models (PCR and LASSO); included in feature weights but no separately reported coefficient or isolated analysis of screen time → non-SM outcome association. Per E4: 'ML — coefficient reported → MET. SHAP/Gini/permutation only → NOT_MET.' Paper reports ML feature importance/weights, not an extractable coefficient. Focus is neurocognition/demographics predicting ADHD, not smartphone/SM as primary exposure with reported stat.

FT_IC3.evidence: FT-IC2 NOT_MET; no inferential test or coefficient specifically linking screen time to non-SM outcome independent of other predictors

FT_IC4.evidence: FT-IC2 NOT_MET; no β/OR/HR/r/CI reported for smartphone/SM association

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: machine learning (PCR and LASSO) models predicting ADHD symptoms from neurocognitive, demographic, and child/family characteristics with cross-validation across ABCD sites

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article in Translational Psychiatry, 2023

FT_EC7_reason: FT-IC2 NOT_MET; screen time not separately analyzed with reported coefficient
notes: Screen time is buried in multi-domain ML model without isolated coefficient. Fails FT-IC2 per E4. Resolver agrees with C2.
